intro: I am interested in the Sr. Data Ops Engineer contract role with Randstad Digital. My strongest fit is the combination of senior data engineering, Python/PySpark pipelines, lakehouse-style architecture, production data operations, and recent hands-on work with RAG, MCP, and AI-assisted workflow automation.
body: At Citi, I built and supported enterprise data pipelines that ingested telemetry from 6,000+ endpoints, transformed it through Python/Pandas and cloud data workflows, and delivered trusted reporting and forecasting outputs for leadership. That work required end-to-end ownership across ingestion, transformation, validation, data quality, troubleshooting, runbooks, and stakeholder-facing analytics in a regulated environment.

The role's focus on Spark-based ingestion, cloud data platforms, lakehouse architecture, governance-aware pipelines, and AI-enabled data capabilities aligns with the direction of my recent work. I have strong hands-on experience with Python, SQL, PySpark, AWS Glue, S3, Redshift, Airflow, data quality, and production support, and I have been actively building AI-assisted data workflows using RAG, FAISS/vector embeddings, MCP tooling, Claude Code, and Grok API integration.

I want to be careful and truthful on fit: my deepest production cloud background is AWS, while the posted role is centered on Google Cloud technologies such as BigQuery, DataProc, Cloud Composer, and GCS. I also treat Kafka, Flink, LangChain, LangGraph, and GraphRAG as current learning or adjacent areas rather than long-term production ownership. Where I can add immediate value is senior data engineering execution, PySpark-oriented pipeline design, operational reliability, AI-assisted automation patterns, and the ability to learn and translate cloud data patterns quickly.
conclusion: I would welcome the opportunity to discuss how my enterprise data engineering and AI pipeline background can help deliver reliable, governed, AI-enabled data capabilities for this team.
